availability.p: © Heidelberger Gesamtverzeichnis der griechischen Papyrusurkunden Ägyptens. This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 License.

sourceDesc.p: The contents of this document are generated from SOSOL.

revisionDesc.change: Uploaded translation from the volume with added line breaks

div.p:
To Apollonius, strategus of Apollonopolites Heptacomia, from Pcheris, son of Pouoris, and Orsenouphis, son of Peteminis, overseers of irrigation for the village of Naboö.
We swear by the fortune of the Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Optimus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus Parthicus that we will make the lands of those named below ready for irrigation within three days. Else may we be liable to our oath.
The names are as follows:
Orsenouphis son of Peteminis 1 aroura
Mersius, son of Horus 1 aroura
The sons of Petosiris, son of Phatres 6 aroura
Hierax, son of Apollonius 1 aroura
The sons of Petosiris 3 3/4 aroura
Mersius, son of Horus 31/32 of an aroura
Pachoumis, son of Pseathyris 15/16 of an aroura
Pachomatres and Orsenouphis, sons of Petosiris 2 1/4 aroura
Pachoumis, son of Petemenecysis 19 arouras
The 2nd year of the Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Optimus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus Parthicus - - -.
Translation: A.C. Johnson, Roman Egypt (1936) no. 5
